things.name: Abteiberg Museum
things.description: Hans Hollein-designed postmodern museum (1982) housing an outstanding collection of Fluxus art, Zero movement works and international contemporary art

things.name: Alter Markt
things.description: The historic market square in the old town with the Romanesque Minster of St. Vitus, dating from the 10th century, and a lively weekend market

things.name: Munster Church of St. Vitus
things.description: Impressive Romanesque minster rising above the old town with a notable crypt, treasury of medieval relics and the town's skyline-defining towers

things.name: Schloss Rheydt
things.description: One of the finest Renaissance moated castles in the Rhineland (1558), housing the city's municipal museum with period interiors and art collections

things.name: Bunter Garten
things.description: Public park in the eastern part of the city with botanical gardens, a children's farm and walking paths around the Bunter Garten lake

things.name: Stadt Museum Monchengladbach
things.description: Municipal history museum tracing the city's development from Roman times through the medieval textile trade to its modern identity

things.name: Schloss Myllendonk
things.description: Medieval moated castle north of Monchengladbach in the Korschenbroich area; a scenic historic landmark surrounded by a working farm estate

things.name: Heyderhof Nature Reserve
things.description: Natural forest and heathland reserve on the city's southern edge with walking and cycling trails through typical Lower Rhine landscape

things.name: Kaiser Friedrich Halle
things.description: Historic concert and events hall at the heart of the city, hosting major performances and civic events throughout the year

things.name: BORUSSIA-PARK
things.description: Home of Borussia Monchengladbach with 54,010 capacity; the club has won five Bundesliga titles, two UEFA Cups and reached the CL 2020-21 knockout rounds
